body:
## Bug

The JavaScript CLI (`@link-assistant/web-capture` on npm) cannot accept any URL as a positional argument. Every invocation fails with:

```
Unknown argument: https://example.com
```

## Root cause

In `js/bin/web-capture.js`, the yargs configuration uses `.strict()` (line ~228) without defining a positional parameter via `.command()`. In strict mode, yargs rejects any argument that isn't a declared option — including the URL that `main()` expects to find in `config._[0]` (line ~828).

## Steps to reproduce

```bash
bun install -g @link-assistant/web-capture@latest
web-capture https://example.com
# Error: Unknown argument: https://example.com

web-capture https://example.com --format markdown
# Error: Unknown argument: https://example.com

web-capture --capture api --archive zip -- https://example.com
# Error: Missing URL or --serve flag
```

All three forms fail. The CLI is completely non-functional for capture mode.

## Suggested fix

Either:

1. **Remove `.strict()`** — simplest fix, allows positional args through.
2. **Define a positional command** — e.g., `.command('$0 [url]', ...)` so yargs knows about the URL positional.

Option 2 is cleaner as it preserves strict validation for typos in option names.

## Environment

- `@link-assistant/web-capture` npm version: latest (reports `0.3.0` internally)
- Node.js: v20.19.4
- Bun: v1.2.20
- macOS (Darwin 24.6.0)

## Notes

- The `--version` flag reports `0.3.0` even though the npm registry shows versions 1.1.2 → 1.7.2 — the internal version string appears out of sync with the published package version.
- The Rust CLI (`web-capture` on crates.io, v0.3.1) works correctly with the same URLs.
title: JS CLI: yargs .strict() rejects positional URL argument
